revision.comment: 51 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:ウマシマジ up to rev 103101433
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 宇摩志麻遅命 | 画像 = Umashimaji.png | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = ウマシマジ（[[菊池容斎]]『[[前賢故実]]』） | 時代 = [[上古]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 可美麻手命、味間見命、味嶋乳命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[宿禰|足尼]]、申食国大夫 | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[物部氏|物部連]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[邇芸速日命]]、母：[[登美夜毘売]] | 兄弟 = [[熟穂屋姫命]]、伊加古夜日売命、一説：[[天香山命]] | 妻 = [[師長姫]] | 子 = [[彦湯支命]]、[[味饒田命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''宇摩志麻遅命'''（うましまぢのみこと）は、[[物部氏]]、[[穂積氏]]、[[采女氏]]らの祖とされる人物。「'''宇摩志麻遅命'''」は『[[古事記]]』での表記。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇摩志麻遅命'''、異表記・異称として『[[日本書紀]]』では'''可美真手命'''（うましまでのみこと）、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''味間見命'''（うましまみのみこと）とされる。
[[ニギハヤヒ|邇芸速日命]]が[[長髄彦|那賀須泥毘古]]の妹である[[ミカシキヤヒメ|登美夜毘売]]<ref>'''三炊屋媛'''（みかしきやひめ）'''長髄媛'''（ながすねびめ）、'''鳥見屋媛'''（とみやびめ）ともいい、'''御炊屋姫'''（『旧事本紀』）とも表記される。</ref>を娶って生んだ子で、『先代旧事本紀』では[[天香山命]]（尾張氏の祖）が異母兄であり、[[彦湯支命]]の父であると伝える。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』によれば、始め那賀須泥毘古に従っていたが、[[神武天皇]]の[[神武東征|東征]]に際して那賀須泥毘古を殺し天皇に帰服し、以後自らの部族である[[物部]]（もののべ）を率いて皇城守護の任に当たったという<ref>以上の業績は、『日本紀』においては饒速日命のそれと伝えられている（神武天皇即位前紀戊午年12月丙申（4日）条）。</ref>。また『旧事本紀』によれば、神武天皇即位の後、饒速日命の遺した10種の天璽瑞宝（あまつしるしのみづたから）を献上し、それを使って天皇と皇后の魂を鎮める呪術を行ったとされ、これを後世の[[鎮魂祭]]の初めとしている。
[[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]の社伝によれば、美濃国・越国を平定した後に石見国で没し、現在の社殿の裏に埋葬されたという。越国の平定は、異母兄の[[天香山命]]の事績として知られる（[[彌彦神社]]を参照）。これら[[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]や[[彌彦神社]]では、宮中でも行われる[[鎮魂祭]]が行われていることでも知られる（[[石上神宮]]も同様）。
「ウマシ」は良いさまや美しい様子を表わす美称で、実名部分は「マチ」「マテ」「マミ」である。「マチ」は亀卜・鹿卜で甲・骨に彫ったり描いたりした形であることからすれば、「ウマシマチ」は卜占の巧みさを表わす名であると考えられる<ref name=黒田>{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[黒田達也]] |year=2014 |title=「天孫本紀」の物部連系譜に関する復元的考察 (I) |journal=大阪府立大学工業高等専門学校研究紀要 |ISSN=2186-2826 |publisher=大阪府立大学工業高等専門学校 |volume=48 |pages=1-14 |doi=10.24729/00007548 |url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>。
「マミ」と「マメ」や、「マチ」「マテ」とは、''i''と''e''の音韻転換である<ref name=黒田 />。
== 祭神とする主な神社 == * [[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]（[[島根県]][[大田市]]）：宇摩志麻遅の没した地とされる * [[味鋺神社]] ([[愛知県]][[名古屋市]])：周囲に[[味鋺古墳群]]を持つ * [[石上神宮]] ([[奈良県]][[天理市]])：宇摩志麻遅の祀った布都御魂剣が、後に宮中から遷されたことで創建された * [[石切剣箭神社]] ([[大阪府]][[東大阪市]])：[[神武天皇即位紀元|皇紀]]2年に生駒山中の宮山に可美真手命が[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日尊]]を奉祀されたのが起源、後に可美真手命が奉祀された
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宮地直一]]・[[佐伯有義]]監修『神道大辞典 縮刷版』、[[臨川書店]]、昭和44年 ISBN 4-653-01347-0
== 関連項目 == *[[浜離宮恩賜庭園]] - [[佐野昭]]作品の銅像が建てられている。 *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[大夫]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[ウマシマジ]] to [[Umashimaji]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 宇摩志麻遅命 | 画像 = Umashimaji.png | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = ウマシマジ（[[菊池容斎]]『[[前賢故実]]』） | 時代 = [[上古]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 可美麻手命、味間見命、味嶋乳命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[宿禰|足尼]]、申食国大夫 | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[穂積氏|穂積臣]]、[[物部氏|物部連]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[邇芸速日命]]、母：[[登美夜毘売]] | 兄弟 = [[熟穂屋姫命]]、伊加古夜日売命、一説：[[天香山命]] | 妻 = [[師長姫]] | 子 = [[彦湯支命]]、[[味饒田命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''宇摩志麻遅命'''（うましまぢのみこと）は、[[物部氏]]、[[穂積氏]]、[[采女氏]]らの祖とされる人物。「'''宇摩志麻遅命'''」は『[[古事記]]』での表記。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇摩志麻遅命'''、異表記・異称として『[[日本書紀]]』では'''可美真手命'''（うましまでのみこと）、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''味間見命'''（うましまみのみこと）とされる。
[[ニギハヤヒ|邇芸速日命]]が[[長髄彦|那賀須泥毘古]]の妹である[[ミカシキヤヒメ|登美夜毘売]]<ref>'''三炊屋媛'''（みかしきやひめ）'''長髄媛'''（ながすねびめ）、'''鳥見屋媛'''（とみやびめ）ともいい、'''御炊屋姫'''（『旧事本紀』）とも表記される。</ref>を娶って生んだ子で、『先代旧事本紀』では[[天香山命]]（尾張氏の祖）が異母兄であり、[[彦湯支命]]の父であると伝える。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』によれば、始め那賀須泥毘古に従っていたが、[[神武天皇]]の[[神武東征|東征]]に際して那賀須泥毘古を殺し天皇に帰服し、以後自らの部族である[[物部]]（もののべ）を率いて皇城守護の任に当たったという<ref>以上の業績は、『日本紀』においては饒速日命のそれと伝えられている（神武天皇即位前紀戊午年12月丙申（4日）条）。</ref>。また『旧事本紀』によれば、神武天皇即位の後、饒速日命の遺した10種の天璽瑞宝（あまつしるしのみづたから）を献上し、それを使って天皇と皇后の魂を鎮める呪術を行ったとされ、これを後世の[[鎮魂祭]]の初めとしている。
[[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]の社伝によれば、美濃国・越国を平定した後に石見国で没し、現在の社殿の裏に埋葬されたという。越国の平定は、異母兄の[[天香山命]]の事績として知られる（[[彌彦神社]]を参照）。これら[[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]や[[彌彦神社]]では、宮中でも行われる[[鎮魂祭]]が行われていることでも知られる（[[石上神宮]]も同様）。
「ウマシ」は良いさまや美しい様子を表わす美称で、実名部分は「マチ」「マテ」「マミ」である。「マチ」は亀卜・鹿卜で甲・骨に彫ったり描いたりした形であることからすれば、「ウマシマチ」は卜占の巧みさを表わす名であると考えられる<ref name=黒田>{{Cite journal|和書|author=[[黒田達也]] |year=2014 |title=「天孫本紀」の物部連系譜に関する復元的考察 (I) |journal=大阪府立大学工業高等専門学校研究紀要 |ISSN=2186-2826 |publisher=大阪府立大学工業高等専門学校 |volume=48 |pages=1-14 |doi=10.24729/00007548 |url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>。
「マミ」と「マメ」や、「マチ」「マテ」とは、''i''と''e''の音韻転換である<ref name=黒田 />。
== 祭神とする主な神社 == * [[物部神社 (大田市)|物部神社]]（[[島根県]][[大田市]]）：宇摩志麻遅の没した地とされる * [[味鋺神社]] ([[愛知県]][[名古屋市]])：周囲に[[味鋺古墳群]]を持つ * [[石上神宮]] ([[奈良県]][[天理市]])：宇摩志麻遅の祀った布都御魂剣が、後に宮中から遷されたことで創建された * [[石切剣箭神社]] ([[大阪府]][[東大阪市]])：[[神武天皇即位紀元|皇紀]]2年に生駒山中の宮山に可美真手命が[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日尊]]を奉祀されたのが起源、後に可美真手命が奉祀された
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宮地直一]]・[[佐伯有義]]監修『神道大辞典 縮刷版』、[[臨川書店]]、昭和44年 ISBN 4-653-01347-0
== 関連項目 == *[[浜離宮恩賜庭園]] - [[佐野昭]]作品の銅像が建てられている。 *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[大夫]]
{{日本神話}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{shinto-stub}}

| spouse = {{ill|Shinaga-hime|ja|師長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| children = {{ill|Hikoyuki no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyuki no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
}}

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

| spouse = {{ill|Shinaga-hime|ja|師長姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.

| parents = Father: {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}; Mother: {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}

| spouse = {{ill|Shinaga-hime|ja|師長姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| children = {{ill|Hikoyuki no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyuki no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命).

According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

Mother: {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}} | siblings = {{ill|Niyaho no Hime|ja|熟穂屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niyaho no Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikakoya-hime|ja|伊加古夜日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikakoya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Possibly {{ill|Amakaga-yama no Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakaga-yama no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} | spouse = {{ill|Shinaga-hime|ja|師長姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | children = {{ill|Hikoyuki no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyuki no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }}
'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.
== Overview ==
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命).
Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>
The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.
According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].
The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>
== Principal Shrines == * [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto. * {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group. * [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined. * {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * [[:en:Taifu|Taifu]]

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]''.

== Overview ==

In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], aiding in his [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Eastern Campaign]]. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]], Umashimaji died in [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami Province]] after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at [[:en:Yahiko Shrine|Yahiko Shrine]] and [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]].

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* [[:en:Mononobe Shrine|Mononobe Shrine]] ([[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōda, Shimane|Ōda]]): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]]): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] ([[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Higashiōsaka|Higashiōsaka]]): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|WD=Q384796|lt=Ōda}}): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka}}): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}}, {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}''.

== Overview ==

In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, aiding in his {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Campaign}}. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|WD=Q384796|lt=Ōda}}): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka}}): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}}, {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}''.

== Overview ==

In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, aiding in his {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Campaign}}. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|WD=Q384796|lt=Ōda}}): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka}}): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province|1=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}''.

== Overview ==

In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, aiding in his {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Campaign|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province|1=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.

{{infobox deity
| name = Umashimaji no Mikoto (宇摩志麻遅命)
| image = Umashimaji.png
| image_size = 
| caption = Umashimaji (from {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Yōsai Kikuchi|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}'s ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'')
| period = [[Ancient Japan]]
| birth_date = Unknown
| death_date = Unknown
| other_names = Kamimade no Mikoto, Ajimami no Mikoto, Ajima Chichino Mikoto
| titles = [[Sukune|Ashini]], Chief of Provincial Governance (申食国大夫)
| allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}
| clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}
| parents = Father: {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}; Mother: {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}
| siblings = {{ill|Niyaho no Hime|ja|熟穂屋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niyaho no Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikakoya-hime|ja|伊加古夜日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikakoya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Possibly {{ill|Amakaga-yama no Mikoto|ja|天香山命|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amakaga-yama no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}}
| spouse = {{ill|Shinaga-hime|ja|師長姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| children = {{ill|Hikoyuki no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyuki no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja|味饒田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajitsukitada no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
}}

'''Umashimaji no Mikoto''' (宇摩志麻遅命) is regarded as the progenitor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Uneme Clan|ja|采女氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme clan|qq=}}. The name "Umashimaji no Mikoto" is used in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}''.

== Overview ==

In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', the name appears as '''Umashimaji no Mikoto''', while the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' uses '''Kamimade no Mikoto''' (可美真手命), and the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' lists it as '''Ajimami no Mikoto''' (味間見命). 

Born to {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|邇芸速日命|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} and {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|登美夜毘売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミカシキヤヒメ|1=Mikashikiya-hime|2=ja|3=ミカシキヤヒメ}}, Umashimaji no Mikoto initially served {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagatsuhiko|lt=Nagasu Negihiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasu Negihiko|qq=}} but later defected to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, aiding in his {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Eastern Campaign|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}. According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', Umashimaji killed Nagasu Negihiko and pledged allegiance to Emperor Jimmu, subsequently leading the Mononobe clan in safeguarding the imperial court.<ref>In the ''Nihon Shoki'', these actions are attributed to Nigihayahi no Mikoto (''Emperor Jimmu, Preface to Accession, 12th Month'').</ref>

The ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' also recounts that after Emperor Jimmu's accession, Umashimaji offered the ten ''amatsushirushi no mitakara'' (天璽瑞宝, heavenly treasures) left behind by Nigihayahi, using them in rituals to calm the spirits of the emperor and empress. This practice became the origin of the modern {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|鎮魂祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}}.

According to the tradition of {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}}, Umashimaji died in {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami Province|1=Iwami Province}} after pacifying the Mino and Koshi regions. His grave is believed to be located behind the shrine's main hall. Similar traditions are also found at {{ill|Yahiko Shrine|en|Yahiko Shrine|WD=Q195684|lt=Yahiko Shrine|1=Yahiko Shrine}} and {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}.

The prefix "Umashi" denotes beauty or excellence, while the root "Machi," "Mate," or "Mami" likely refers to divinatory skill, suggesting a connection to divination practices.<ref name=kuroda>{{Cite journal|author=Tatsuya Kuroda|title=Restorative Studies on the Genealogy of the Mononobe Clan in the ''Tenson Honki'' (Part I)|journal=Research Bulletin of Osaka Prefecture University Technical College|volume=48|pages=1–14|doi=10.24729/00007548|year=2014|url=https://doi.org/10.24729/00007548}}</ref>

== Principal Shrines ==
* {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|WD=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}} ({{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōda, Shimane|en|Ōda, Shimane|WD=Q384796|lt=Ōda|1=Ōda, Shimane}}): The supposed burial site of Umashimaji no Mikoto.
* {{ill|Ajima Shrine|ja|味鋺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}): Located near the Ajima Kofun Group.
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} ({{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}): Founded after the transfer of the Futsumitama Sword, which Umashimaji enshrined.
* {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiōsaka|en|Higashiōsaka|WD=Q243863|lt=Higashiōsaka|1=Higashiōsaka}}): Established by Umashimaji in honor of Nigihayahi.
